basic.name: simple_select
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 18;

basic.name: basic_insert
basic.sql: INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('John Doe', 'john@example.com', NOW());

basic.name: basic_update
basic.sql: UPDATE users SET status = 'active' WHERE created_at > '2024-01-01';

basic.name: simple_join
basic.sql: SELECT u.name, o.order_id FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id;

basic.name: group_by_aggregation
basic.sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) as count FROM products GROUP BY category;

basic.name: basic_where_and
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100 AND category = 'Electronics';

basic.name: order_by_limit
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;

basic.name: count_aggregate
basic.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed';

basic.name: sum_aggregate
basic.sql: SELECT SUM(amount) as total_revenue FROM orders WHERE order_date >= '2024-01-01';

basic.name: avg_aggregate
basic.sql: SELECT AVG(price) as avg_price FROM products GROUP BY category;

basic.name: in_clause
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE role IN ('admin', 'manager', 'supervisor');

basic.name: like_pattern
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%laptop%';

basic.name: between_clause
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-12-31';

basic.name: is_null_check
basic.sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE last_login IS NULL;

basic.name: distinct_select
basic.sql: SELECT DISTINCT category FROM products;

basic.name: max_min_aggregate
basic.sql: SELECT MAX(price) as max_price, MIN(price) as min_price FROM products;

basic.name: having_clause
basic.sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) as count FROM products GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 10;

basic.name: basic_delete
basic.sql: DELETE FROM temp_data WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';

basic.name: create_table_basic
alter_table_add_column
basic.sql: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20);

intermediate.name: subquery_in
intermediate.sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE amount > 1000);

intermediate.name: cte_basic
intermediate.sql:
WITH high_value_customers AS (
    SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) as total
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING SUM(amount) > 5000
)
SELECT u.name, hvc.total FROM users u
JOIN high_value_customers hvc ON u.id = hvc.user_id;

intermediate.name: window_function_rank
multiple_joins
intermediate.sql:
SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) as order_count, SUM(o.amount) as total_spent
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id
GROUP BY u.id, u.name
HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5;

intermediate.name: case_statement
intermediate.sql:
SELECT name,
    CASE 
        WHEN salary > 100000 THEN 'Executive'
        WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'Senior'
        ELSE 'Junior'
    END as level
FROM employees;

intermediate.name: coalesce_function
intermediate.sql: SELECT name, COALESCE(phone, email, 'No contact') as contact FROM users;

intermediate.name: string_aggregation
intermediate.sql: SELECT department, STRING_AGG(name, ', ' ORDER BY name) as employees FROM employees GROUP BY department;

intermediate.name: jsonb_operations
intermediate.sql:
SELECT id, 
    data->>'customer_name' as customer,
    CAST(data->'amount' as DECIMAL) as amount
FROM transactions
WHERE data @> '{"status": "completed"}';

intermediate.name: array_operations
intermediate.sql: SELECT id, name FROM products WHERE 'electronics' = ANY(tags);

intermediate.name: date_truncation
intermediate.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) as month, SUM(amount) as revenue FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date);

intermediate.name: self_join
intermediate.sql: SELECT e1.name as employee, e2.name as manager FROM employees e1 LEFT JOIN employees e2 ON e1.manager_id = e2.id;

intermediate.name: union_queries
exists_clause
intermediate.sql: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id AND o.amount > 1000);

intermediate.name: window_lead_lag
intermediate.sql:
SELECT date, revenue,
    LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY date) as previous_day_revenue,
    LEAD(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY date) as next_day_revenue
FROM daily_sales;

intermediate.name: cross_join
intermediate.sql: SELECT p.name, c.color FROM products p CROSS JOIN colors c;

intermediate.name: full_outer_join
intermediate.sql: SELECT * FROM table1 t1 FULL OUTER JOIN table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;

intermediate.name: extract_date_parts
intermediate.sql: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) as year, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) as month, SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY 1, 2;

intermediate.name: conditional_aggregation
intermediate.sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as completed_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'pending' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) as pending_revenue
FROM orders;

intermediate.name: row_number_pagination
regexp_matching
intermediate.sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email ~ '^[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}$';

advanced.name: recursive_cte
advanced.sql:
WITH RECURSIVE org_hierarchy AS (
    SELECT id, name, manager_id, 1 as level
    FROM employees
    WHERE manager_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, h.level + 1
    FROM employees e
    JOIN org_hierarchy h ON e.manager_id = h.id
)
SELECT * FROM org_hierarchy ORDER BY level, name;

advanced.name: multiple_ctes
advanced.sql:
WITH monthly_sales AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) as month, SUM(amount) as total
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)
),
yearly_sales AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('year', order_date) as year, SUM(amount) as total
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('year', order_date)
)
SELECT m.month, m.total as monthly, y.total as yearly
FROM monthly_sales m
JOIN yearly_sales y ON DATE_TRUNC('year', m.month) = y.year;

advanced.name: window_moving_average
advanced.sql:
SELECT date, revenue,
    AVG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as moving_avg_7d,
    SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as rolling_sum_30d
FROM daily_sales;

advanced.name: lateral_join
advanced.sql:
SELECT u.name, recent_orders.*
FROM users u
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT o.id, o.amount, o.order_date
    FROM orders o
    WHERE o.user_id = u.id
    ORDER BY o.order_date DESC
    LIMIT 5
) recent_orders;

advanced.name: pivot_crosstab
percentile_calculation
advanced.sql:
SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary) as p25,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary) as median,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary) as p75,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.90) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary) as p90
FROM employees;

advanced.name: generate_series_dates
advanced.sql:
SELECT dates.day, COALESCE(COUNT(o.id), 0) as order_count
FROM generate_series(
    '2024-01-01'::date,
    '2024-12-31'::date,
    '1 day'::interval
) AS dates(day)
LEFT JOIN orders o ON dates.day = o.order_date::date
GROUP BY dates.day
ORDER BY dates.day;

advanced.name: full_text_search
advanced.sql:
SELECT id, title, ts_rank(to_tsvector('english', content), to_tsquery('english', 'database & optimization')) as rank
FROM articles
WHERE to_tsvector('english', content) @@ to_tsquery('english', 'database & optimization')
ORDER BY rank DESC;

advanced.name: complex_nested_query
advanced.sql:
SELECT u.name, u.email,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as total_orders,
    (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as total_spent,
    (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as avg_order
FROM users u
WHERE u.id IN (
    SELECT user_id FROM orders
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING SUM(amount) > 1000
)
ORDER BY total_spent DESC;

advanced.name: upsert_on_conflict
advanced.sql:
INSERT INTO user_stats (user_id, order_count, total_spent, last_updated)
VALUES (123, 1, 99.99, NOW())
ON CONFLICT (user_id)
DO UPDATE SET
    order_count = user_stats.order_count + 1,
    total_spent = user_stats.total_spent + 99.99,
    last_updated = NOW();

intermediate.name: left_join_with_aggregation
intermediate.sql: SELECT c.name, COALESCE(SUM(o.amount), 0) as total_spent FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name;

intermediate.name: case_when
intermediate.sql: SELECT name, CASE WHEN salary > 100000 THEN 'High' WHEN salary > 50000 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END as salary_band FROM employees;

advanced.name: window_function_row_number
recursive_cte
advanced.sql:
WITH RECURSIVE category_tree AS (
    SELECT id, name, parent_id, 1 as level
    FROM categories
    WHERE parent_id IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT c.id, c.name, c.parent_id, ct.level + 1
    FROM categories c
    INNER JOIN category_tree ct ON c.parent_id = ct.id
)
SELECT * FROM category_tree;

advanced.name: complex_join_aggregation
advanced.sql:
SELECT p.name, 
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) as order_count,
    SUM(oi.quantity) as total_quantity,
    AVG(oi.price) as avg_price
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id
LEFT JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id
WHERE o.order_date >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
GROUP BY p.id, p.name
HAVING order_count > 10;

dialect_specific.name: fulltext_search
dialect_specific.sql: SELECT id, title FROM articles WHERE MATCH(content) AGAINST('database optimization' IN BOOLEAN MODE);

dialect_specific.name: on_duplicate_key
dialect_specific.sql:
INSERT INTO user_stats (user_id, order_count, total_spent)
VALUES (123, 1, 99.99)
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
    order_count = order_count + 1,
    total_spent = total_spent + 99.99;

advanced.name: cte_with_ranking
pivot_table
merge_statement
advanced.sql:
MERGE INTO target_table AS t
USING source_table AS s
ON t.id = s.id
WHEN MATCHED THEN
    UPDATE SET t.value = s.value, t.updated_at = GETDATE()
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
    INSERT (id, value, created_at) VALUES (s.id, s.value, GETDATE());

advanced.name: analytical_functions
advanced.sql:
SELECT name, salary, department,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) as dept_rank,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) as overall_rank,
    LEAD(salary) OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary) as next_salary
FROM employees;

advanced.name: stream_processing
cte_with_window
advanced.sql:
WITH monthly_sales AS (
    SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) as month, 
           SUM(amount) as total,
           LAG(SUM(amount)) OVER (ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)) as prev_month
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)
)
SELECT month, total, prev_month,
       ((total - prev_month) / prev_month * 100) as growth_percent
FROM monthly_sales;
